title: CIViC ETL -> Biolink edges with full provenance + evidence verbatim
context: CIViC (CC0) is the first approved source for the knowledge graph. An ETL pipeline must transform a pinned, checksummed CIViC release into Biolink associations with complete provenance. Evidence levels (A-E) and AMP/ASCO/CAP tiers must be carried verbatim, never re-graded. Variants must be mapped to GA4GH VRS identifiers. This is the end-to-end proof of the extraction architecture. Depends on CI (oncogene-kg-ci-001) and license analysis (oncogene-kg-license-002).
objective: Implement a CIViC-to-Biolink ETL pipeline that ingests a pinned, checksummed CIViC release, maps variants to GA4GH VRS, carries evidence levels and AMP/ASCO/CAP tiers verbatim, attaches complete provenance to every edge, and passes all CI gates.
acceptanceCriteria: Transforms a pinned, checksummed CIViC release to Biolink associations; the pinned version and checksum are hardcoded or stored in the pipeline config.
CIViC evidence levels (A-E) and AMP/ASCO/CAP tiers are carried verbatim and the pipeline cannot re-grade them.
Variants are mapped to GA4GH VRS identifiers; source-native CIViC variant IDs are retained as aliases.
Every edge carries a complete provenance record: source=CIViC, record ID, license=CC0, version, PMID, method, pipeline commit hash.
Ambiguous or unmappable variants are flagged, never silently dropped or invented.
Output passes Biolink/SHACL validation and all CI gates (provenance-completeness + allow-list linter).
output: etl/civic/ directory containing the CIViC ETL pipeline code and config (pinned version/checksum), committed via PR, with CI green.
